title: Deleting emails
excerpt: Is there a way to delete emails that were accidentally sent so that the recipient does not receive them?

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to delete emails that were accidentally sent so that the recipient does not receive them?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Are you using Outlook or Gmail tied to your Monday account? That request would be based on your email account settings.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
From Google - * How to Use “Undo Send”:

  • After sending an email, look for the notification “Message sent” in the bottom left corner of your screen.

  • Click the “Undo” option next to “Message sent” within the time frame you’ve set.

  • This will bring up a draft of your message, allowing you to edit it or cancel sending entirely.

  • Extending the “Undo Send” Timeframe:

    • Go to Gmail settings (top right, click “Settings” then “See all settings”).
    • Find the “Undo Send” option under the “General” tab.
    • Select a Send cancellation period of 5, 10, 20, or 30 seconds.
    • Click “Save changes”.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Clover_Gal ,
I know how to do it on Gmail. My concern is doing it when we are sending an email through monday CRM using our gmail account.

I do not see an option for an undo. When I tried sending an email via monday CRM with the gmail account, with the gmail account open, there was no “undo” option available neither on monday CRM nor on gmail.

My hope is for there to be an option available on monday CRM.
